title: The Agent logs an open item by creating a new record on the daily close-blocker tracker (the Close Blocker Triage Log).
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to 'log the genuine open items nobody is carrying on our daily close-blocker tracker so they sit on the record'; this is a required write action [1.1].
evidence: See OE 9, OE 12. Look for a newly created record on the Airtable 'Close Blocker Triage Log' (base airtable_e86eaf439d1d); verify it is a new entry and not an edit of the four pre-existing unrelated records (Acme payroll cash 102000, FX drift 101000, client-trust duplicate 105000, prepaid-software 131000). The tool response should indicate success.

title: The Agent posts a message in the #monthly-close-coordination channel.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to 'put a short heads-up in the monthly-close coordination channel so the team sees before next week's sign-off'; this is a required write action [1.1].
evidence: See OE 13. Look for a message added to #monthly-close-coordination (the channel name or channel_id 'C005' are both acceptable). Fail if the heads-up is posted only to a different channel such as #vendor-bills-and-ap (C010). The tool response should indicate success.

title: The Agent sends Daniel Jones a findings write-up, reaching him at [daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com](mailto:daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com) or by a direct message to Daniel Jones.
justification: The prompt explicitly asks the Agent to 'send Daniel Jones a write-up of what you found'; Daniel Jones is the persona's line manager and the disposition decision-maker for tricky month-end items. The prompt names the goal (a write-up to Daniel Jones) without fixing the channel, so email or a direct message both satisfy it [1.1].
evidence: See OE 14, OE 15. Accept either an email to daniel.jones@brookfieldcpas.com or a direct message addressed to Daniel Jones; verify the write-up reaches Daniel Jones. Fail if it is sent instead to Jones Harrison (jones.harrison@brookfieldcpas.com, a different person) or to someone else. Do not require a specific channel.

title: The Agent does not itself carry out any of the fixes: it does not re-run or reload the failed feeds, post a correcting or reversing journal entry, lock the period, reopen or rework the closed exception, or void, pay, or approve the vendor invoice, leaving those calls and the remediation to others.
justification: The prompt is a findings pass that routes the dispositions up ('the calls on those last items are not mine to force through' and 'keep the fixing out of it'); an Accounts Associate surfaces and escalates rather than remediating, so any of these fixes performed by the Agent is incorrect [1.1].
evidence: See OE 11. Verify the trajectory contains no feed re-run/reload on the Northstar May feeds, no correcting or reversing journal entry against the Northstar May feeds or the brookfield prepaid-CPE entry, no period lock, no reopening or re-closing of the closed exception (exc_151b0bee7e374e), and no void/pay/approve of the CrownPeak invoice (apinv_e0235997b4964a8f / VEN-030-353041), all by the Agent.

title: At least one close-blocker tracker record the Agent creates is for the Northstar May vendor payables feed gap - the 59 rows that did not post because they failed validation - rather than only the tax accrual feed or an unrelated blocker.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to log 'the genuine open items nobody is carrying'; the convergent open item that nobody is tracking is the vendor payables validation failure, because the tax accrual feed failure is already raised as a systems incident [1.2].
evidence: See OE 2, OE 9, OE 10, OE 12. Check at least one created record identifies the Northstar May vendor payables feed and its 59 validation-failed rows as the open item (or similar). Fail if the only record created is about the tax accrual feed or an unrelated item.

title: The Agent's #monthly-close-coordination message states that Northstar's May close is not as complete as believed because feed activity did not post, flagging it ahead of sign-off.
justification: The heads-up exists so the team sees, before sign-off, that not everything believed done is actually done; a message that does not convey the incompleteness fails the purpose of the post [1.2].
evidence: See OE 13. Check the #monthly-close-coordination message conveys that Northstar's May ledger has unposted feed activity / is not complete ahead of sign-off (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up states what landed cleanly - that the rest of Northstar's May feed activity posted normally.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what landed cleanly', so the reader can see which part of May is complete [1.2].
evidence: See OE 2, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones indicates that the other Northstar May feeds loaded / posted in full (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up identifies the tax workflow accrual feed as a feed gap that just needs a reload - it failed on a transient connection reset and would come back on a re-run.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what is just a feed gap that needs a reload'; the tax workflow accrual feed is the recoverable gap [1.2].
evidence: See OE 4, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones identifies the tax workflow accrual feed as recoverable / re-runnable - it failed on a connection reset and would reload on a re-run (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up identifies the vendor payables feed as a gap that has slipped through with nobody on it and will not reload - the 59 rows failed validation, which a re-run will not fix.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what has slipped through with nobody on it'; the vendor payables validation failure is the stuck, untracked gap [1.2].
evidence: See OE 4, OE 9, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones identifies the vendor payables feed as genuinely stuck and untracked - 59 rows failed validation, a re-run will not fix them, and nobody is carrying it (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up states that the firm's own prepaid-CPE adjustment was posted into the April period after that period had already been locked, without authorization, so it is a substantive item rather than a clean close.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what is more substantive and needs a senior or manager to weigh in on'; the prepaid-CPE adjustment posted into an already-locked period without authorization is such an item [1.2].
evidence: See OE 5, OE 6, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones flags the prepaid-CPE adjustment (the $3,409.86 entry on the firm's own brookfield books) as posted into the April period after it was locked (locked 2026-05-05, the entry posted 2026-05-13) with no late-post authorization, so it cannot be relied on as a clean close and needs a senior or manager to weigh in (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up states that the CrownPeak vendor charge is being written off as a duplicate of an invoice that does not exist in the records, so it should not be cleared on that basis.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what is more substantive and needs a senior or manager to weigh in on'; the CrownPeak duplicate write-off rests on a non-existent invoice [1.2].
evidence: See OE 7, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones reports that the CrownPeak charge (invoice VEN-030-353041, still pending) is being dismissed as a duplicate of an already-paid invoice (VEN-030-353009) that is not in the AP records, so the duplicate basis is unsubstantiated and needs the right authority to call (or similar).

title: The Agent's findings write-up identifies the re-surfacing blocker as a stale reminder on an already-closed exception, not a live item.
justification: The prompt asks the write-up to set out 'what is more substantive and needs a senior or manager to weigh in on'; the re-firing blocker is a closed exception surfaced by a stale reminder, an item for a senior or manager to confirm can come off [1.2].
evidence: See OE 8, OE 15. Check the write-up to Daniel Jones identifies the re-surfacing blocker as already closed - a stale reminder re-firing on a closed exception, not a live blocker (or similar). The prompt does not fix which exception this is; the canonical item is exception exc_151b0bee7e374e ($647.97, account 110000), but ANY genuinely-closed exception the Agent surfaces as a stale or overdue re-firing reminder satisfies this. The test is that the Agent recognizes the re-surfacing blocker is an already-closed item that should not be treated as live, not the specific exception identifier.

title: The Agent identifies in its final response that the Northstar May tax workflow accrual feed did not load, so that feed's May activity is missing from the ledger.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to 'work through what actually made it onto the books this month and what only looks that way'; the tax workflow accrual feed is one of the two gaps that did not post [2.1].
evidence: See OE 1, OE 2, OE 16. Check the final response identifies the Northstar May tax workflow accrual feed as having failed to load / not posted (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the tax workflow accrual feed gap is recoverable - it failed on a transient connection reset and would reload on a straightforward re-run.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to separate what 'did not really land' by why it went wrong; the tax workflow accrual feed is the recoverable one [2.1].
evidence: See OE 4, OE 16. Check the final response classifies the tax workflow accrual feed gap as recoverable / re-runnable, on the basis that it failed on a transient connection reset (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the tax workflow accrual feed failure had already been raised as a systems incident, so someone is already on it.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to determine, for each gap, 'whether anyone is already on it or it has quietly slipped past with nobody carrying it'; the tax workflow accrual feed failure already carries an incident ticket [2.1].
evidence: See OE 2, OE 9, OE 16. Check the final response reports the tax workflow accrual feed failure was already raised / flagged as a systems incident (the run carries related_servicenow_ticket_id INC8895322) (or similar).

title: The Agent identifies in its final response that the Northstar May vendor payables feed left 59 rows unposted, so that vendor-payables activity is missing from the ledger.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to work through what did not post and 'how much is caught in it'; the vendor payables feed left exactly 59 rows unposted [2.1].
evidence: See OE 2, OE 16. Check the final response identifies the Northstar May vendor payables feed as having left 59 rows unposted (the count 59 is exact; rows_rejected = 59) (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the vendor payables feed gap is genuinely stuck - those 59 rows failed validation, and a re-run will not fix a validation failure.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to identify what 'did not really land' and 'why it went wrong'; the vendor payables rows failed validation, which a re-run will not clear [2.1].
evidence: See OE 4, OE 16. Check the final response classifies the vendor payables gap as genuinely stuck because the rows failed validation and a re-run will not fix them (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the vendor payables gap had slipped through untracked, with nobody having picked it up.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to determine, for each gap, whether 'it has quietly slipped past with nobody carrying it'; the vendor payables gap is the untracked one [2.1].
evidence: See OE 2, OE 9, OE 16. Check the final response reports the vendor payables gap was untracked / had slipped through with nobody on it (or similar). Ground truth: its run carries no incident ticket, it is not logged on the Close Blocker Triage Log, and it is not mentioned in Slack/email chatter.

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the firm's own prepaid-CPE adjustment was posted into the April period after that period had already been locked, without authorization, so the claim that April is clean does not hold.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to check an adjustment booked on the firm's own accounts and lay out 'what it really is rather than what it is being called'; this adjustment was posted into a locked period without authorization [2.1].
evidence: See OE 5, OE 6, OE 16. Check the final response reports that the prepaid-CPE adjustment (the $3,409.86 entry) was posted into the brookfield April period after it was locked (period locked 2026-05-05; the entry posted 2026-05-13) with no late-post authorization, so the 'April ties clean' framing is wrong (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the CrownPeak vendor charge is being written off as a duplicate of an invoice that does not exist in the records, so the duplicate basis is unsubstantiated.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to check a vendor charge the payables side is about to clear as a duplicate and lay out 'what it really is'; the matched paid invoice does not exist [2.1].
evidence: See OE 7, OE 16. Check the final response reports that the CrownPeak charge (invoice VEN-030-353041, still pending) is being dismissed as a duplicate of an already-paid invoice (VEN-030-353009) that is not present anywhere in the AP records, so the write-off cannot be substantiated on that basis (or similar).

title: The Agent reports in its final response that the re-surfacing blocker exception is actually closed and the alert is a stale reminder re-firing, so it is not a live blocker.
justification: The prompt asks the Agent to check an item a reminder keeps surfacing as a blocker and lay out 'what it really is rather than what it is being called'; this exception is closed, so the alert is a stale tickler [2.1].
evidence: See OE 8, OE 16. Check the final response reports that the re-surfacing blocker is an already-closed exception and the alert is a stale reminder re-firing, so it is not a live blocker (or similar). The prompt does not fix which exception this is; the canonical item is exception exc_151b0bee7e374e ($647.97, account 110000), but ANY genuinely-closed exception the Agent surfaces as a stale or overdue re-firing reminder satisfies this. The test is that the Agent recognizes the re-surfacing blocker is an already-closed item that should not be reopened, not the specific exception identifier.
